findings.text: The blood ran bright for a moment, then went black where the salt dried it into the grain of the wood.
findings.deletionTest: The blood ran bright, then went black where the salt dried it into the grain of the wood.
findings.suggestedFix: The blood ran bright, then went black where the salt dried it into the grain of the wood.

findings.text: Warm country was harder on Sylvanas than the cold had been.
findings.suggestedFix: Warm damp sat wrong on Sylvanas.

findings.text: The mage had stopped treating care as interruption.
findings.whatItRestates: The shifted ranger spacing around Jaina and Jaina's silent acceptance of it.

findings.text: Antonidas looked at her then, and fear slipped past the archmage for a beat before work buried it again.
findings.deletionTest: Antonidas looked at her then, and fear slipped past the archmage before work buried it again.
findings.suggestedFix: Antonidas looked at her then, and fear slipped past the archmage before work buried it again.

findings.text: It shut with the sound of the last help in the kingdom staying outside.

registerSummaries.registerSummary: Jaina thinks in magical mechanism, bodily cost, and clipped analytical fragments when the seam in Frostmourne clarifies. Even exhausted, her interior lane stays practical and problem-solving rather than essayistic or thematic.

Sylvanas tracks spacing, readiness, timing, and social weakness in hard tactical shorthand. Her close-third lane is unsentimental and incisive, but it still cashes out through field logic rather than epigraph fragments or thematic commentary.

findings.text: The body handled it. The temper did not.
findings.povMismatch: This reads like a slogan pinned over Sylvanas's discomfort instead of a tactical thought occurring inside her ride-time attention.

findings.text: He was no longer arriving. He was settled.
findings.povMismatch: The runner's update is already concrete; this compresses it into a caption instead of letting Sylvanas stay in timeline math.

findings.text: They were living with the truth available to them, which was a worse innocence than stupidity.
findings.povMismatch: Sylvanas can condemn the village's ignorance, but this sentence does it in polished thematic prose rather than her sharper field register.
findings.suggestedFix: They were cheering the story they'd been given.

findings.text: No cruelty in it. Mere fact.
findings.povMismatch: The quote already lands; the follow-up fragments caption it from above instead of staying inside Sylvanas's active read.

findings.text: It shut with the sound of the last help in the kingdom staying outside.
findings.povMismatch: This comments on the threshold's narrative significance instead of limiting itself to what Sylvanas registers as the door closes.

findings.text: the body keeping count under everything else presented its bill.
findings.missingPayload: The accumulated sail-pushing exhaustion landing in Jaina's legs the instant she stops feeding the wind.
findings.reason: Placeholder-noun phrasing hides the concrete collapse mechanism behind metaphorical bookkeeping.
findings.suggestedFix: The moment she stopped feeding the sails, the spent strength hit all at once.

findings.text: the truth available to them, which was a worse innocence than stupidity.
findings.failedQuestion: What does this cash out to?
findings.missingPayload: The village celebrating the lie that Arthas came home a hero instead of carrying the danger back with him.
findings.reason: The sentence wants the weight of moral precision without naming the actual false story these villagers are living under.
findings.suggestedFix: They were cheering the story they'd been given.

findings.text: What showed instead was a man hearing the price attached to one particular body.
findings.missingPayload: Antonidas realizing Sylvanas is the body that has to stand in Frostmourne's reach.
findings.reason: The line sounds exact but dodges the actual person and realization that make the reaction legible.
findings.suggestedFix: What showed instead was fear when he looked at Sylvanas.

findings.text: The ordinary machinery of a palace breathing on the far side of murder.
findings.missingPayload: Kitchen work and servant life still carrying on just beyond the court.
findings.reason: The line reaches for atmosphere instead of naming the ordinary labor that is still going on nearby.
findings.suggestedFix: Kitchen work went on beyond the court.

audits.cleanReason: The one translation setup ('Tell me the plan in words a judge could follow.') cashes out into usable sequence, timing, and proximity. Antonidas needs a follow-up about who buys the seconds, not what the plain-language plan means.

summary.verdictReason: Fail: 14 mild sentence-level kills across three pass-B guides exceeds the pass threshold of 3 mild-only kills.
